title: Why Training Needs Teacher Forcing
concept: Without teacher forcing, an early mistake would derail the rest of the generation, making the model learn from incorrect context and slowing convergence.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of using 'Teacher Forcing' during model training?

options.text: To keep the model on track by feeding it the correct next word during training.
To make the model run faster on older computer hardware.
To delete bad training data automatically from the database.

exercises.explain: Teacher forcing is used during the training phase of sequence-to-sequence models to speed up convergence by feeding correct target words.

cards.title: The Danger of Early Mistakes
cards.body: Imagine trying to learn a long dance routine. If you make a mistake on the very first step, and nobody corrects you, every step you take after that will be completely wrong! In AI training, a single early mistake can derail the entire learning process.

cards.title: What is Teacher Forcing?
cards.body: Teacher Forcing is a training method where we feed the model the correct 'ground truth' words from the training data at each step, rather than letting it feed its own potentially incorrect guesses back into itself. This keeps the model on the right track during training.

cards.title: The Piano Teacher
cards.body: Imagine teaching a child to play a song on the piano. If they hit a wrong note at the very beginning, and you let them keep playing, the entire song becomes a mess of noise. Instead, you instantly place their finger on the correct key so they can learn how the rest of the song is actually supposed to sound.

cards.title: Correcting on the Fly
cards.body: If a model tries to translate 'I love cats' to French, it might start with 'Je' (correct) but then guess 'hate' (incorrect) for the second word. With teacher forcing, we immediately replace 'hate' with the correct word 'adore' before it predicts the third word. This prevents one mistake from ruining the entire sentence.

cards.title: Letting it Stumble?
cards.body: Many believe that letting a model learn from its own mistakes during early training is the fastest way to learn. In reality, letting a beginner model stumble blindly on its own bad outputs leads to a feedback loop of gibberish, making training take forever or fail completely!

cards.title: Training Paths compared
cards.body: See how teacher forcing guides the model step-by-step compared to letting it wander off track on its own.

right.text: Step 1: Predicts 'He' (Correct)\nStep 2: Predicts 'are' -> Replaced with 'is' (Corrected)\nStep 3: Uses 'is' -> Predicts 'running' (On Track)

exercises.prompt: What happens if a sequence model makes an early mistake during training without teacher forcing?

options.text: The model will train too quickly and overshoot the target.
The model will learn from its own wrong predictions, compounding its mistakes.
The model will run out of memory and crash instantly.
The model will only output numbers instead of words.

exercises.explain: Without teacher forcing, the model feeds its own incorrect guesses back into itself, causing errors to accumulate and preventing the model from learning the correct sequence patterns.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Without teacher forcing, training sequence models takes much longer because the model gets stuck learning from its own incorrect predictions.
exercises.explain: Teacher forcing is crucial for training sequence models because it stops errors from accumulating, allowing the model to converge much faster.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe what happens to sequence generation without teacher forcing.
exercises.sentence: Without teacher forcing, an early mistake can easily derail the entire sequence.
exercises.explain: Without teacher forcing, a single mistake early in a sequence will derail the rest of the generation because the model will generate new words based on its own bad history.

exercises.prompt:
Two models try to translate 'I love apples because they are sweet'.

Sentence A: 'J'aime les pommes parce qu'elles sont sucrées.'
Sentence B: 'Je déteste les pommes parce qu'elles sont horribles et mauvaises.'

Which statement is correct?

options.text: Both sentences are generated with teacher forcing.
Sentence B shows a model that got derailed by an early mistake and spiraled.
Sentence A shows a model that is completely broken.

exercises.explain: Option A is generated by a model trained with teacher forcing because it stays on track. Option B represents a model without teacher forcing that got derailed by its own early mistake (predicting 'hate' instead of 'love') and spiraled into negative gibberish.

exercises.prompt: Why does training a sequence model without Teacher Forcing often lead to very slow or failed learning?

options.text: It feeds the model its own incorrect predictions during training, causing errors to pile up.
It forces the model to use too much memory by loading the entire dataset at once.
It prevents the model from using GPU acceleration during the backpropagation step.
It automatically deletes the model weights if the accuracy drops below a threshold.

exercises.explain: Without teacher forcing, a model's early mistakes cascade, causing it to generate wild gibberish. The model ends up trying to learn from its own bad outputs, which makes training extremely slow and unstable.
